subject: [PATCH 07/10] NFSD: check truncate permission under inode lock

2026-05-28.patch_summary: This patch addresses a TOCTOU (Time-of-Check, Time-of-Use) vulnerability in the NFSD code by ensuring that truncate permission checks are performed under inode lock. The patch splits the write-count acquisition from the truncation permission check and rechecks whether the requested size is below the locked file size after acquiring the inode lock. This change prevents concurrent appends from extending the file before the NFSD MAY_TRUNC check, which rejects append-only files. The patch also avoids loading the unlocked inode->i_size value.
